1	# ASX Release
2	27 February 2024
3	
4	## 1H24 Record Results
5	
6	NEXTDC Limited (ASX: NXT) (“NEXTDC” or “the Company”) today announced its financial results for the halfyear ended 31 December 2023 (“1H24”).
7	
8	**1H24 financial highlights**
9	
10	 Total revenue increased A$49.4 million (31%) to A$209.1 million (1H23: A$159.7 million)
11	
12	-  Net revenue[1] up A$10.7 million (8%) to A$149.1 million (1H23: A$138.4 million)
13	
14	-  Higher power prices and related power passthrough revenues positively impacting total revenue
15	
16	 Underlying EBITDA[2,3] increased A$4.5 million (5%) to A$102.0 million (1H23: A$97.5 million)
17	
18	 Invested A$220 million to progress capital development projects (1H23: A$330 million)
19	
20	 Liquidity (cash and undrawn debt facilities) of A$2.1 billion at 31 December 2023[4]
21	
22	NEXTDC Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director, Craig Scroggie, commented on the 1H24 results:
23	
24	_“We are pleased to deliver another record result in 1H24, with the business continuing to exhibit solid growth_
25	_across key metrics during this reporting period._
26	
27	_The Company remains well capitalised to take advantage of its strong forward sales pipeline as well as continue to_
28	_build its forward sales and earnings outlook._
29	
30	_As demand continues to be bolstered by the broad adoption of new technologies such as generative AI, the_
31	_business remains in an outstanding position to support customer growth requirements across the Enterprise,_
32	_Government and Hyperscale verticals.”_
33	
34	**Business performance**
35	
36	For the 12 months to 31 December 2023:
37	
38	 Contracted utilisation increased 64.8MW[5] (77%) to 149.0MW
39	
40	-  Record forward order book of 68.8MW[6] is projected to ramp into billing across FY25 to FY29, underpinning
41	
42	future growth in revenues and earnings
43	
44	 Customer numbers up by 218 (13%) to 1,919 (31 December 2022: 1,701)
45	
46	 Interconnections[7] grew 906 (5%) to 18,207, representing 9.2% of net revenue
47	
48	1 Comprises total revenue less direct costs
49	2 EBITDA is a non-statutory financial metric representing earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation. Non-statutory
50	financial metrics have been extracted from the interim financial report
51	3 Refer to page 27 of the 1H24 Results Presentation for a reconciliation to Underlying EBITDA
52	4 Reflects 31 December 2023 cash position of $589 million and a further $1.5 billion of undrawn capacity
53	5 Represents increase in utilisation over the 12-month period from 31 December 2022 to 31 December 2023
54	6 Forward order book represents the difference between the contracted utilisation (149.0MW) and billing utilisation (80.2MW) at the end
55	of 1H24
56	7 Comprises both physical and elastic cross connects
57	
58	
59	-----
60	
61	**Development activity**
62	
63	 4MW of built capacity was added to S3 Sydney, with 20MW in progress and a further 20MW of capacity now in
64	
65	planning
66	
67	 M2 Melbourne added 3MW of built capacity, with 12MW in progress and a further 15MW in planning
68	
69	 PH1 Port Hedland opened to customers with 0.5MW of built capacity
70	
71	 Development works continue for new regions A1 Adelaide, D1 Darwin and NE1 Newman, with A1 Adelaide
72	
73	expected to open to customers in 1H25
74	
75	 Digital Transformation Agency (DTA) Certified Strategic status granted for S3 Sydney & M3 Melbourne, putting
76	
77	these sites in a stronger position to serve government clients
78	
79	 Smart cooling system upgrades now installed in S1 Sydney, M1 Melbourne and P1 Perth to deliver more
80	
81	efficient winter cooling performance from these facilities
82	
83	**FY24 guidance reaffirmed**
84	
85	Based on current billing, contracted utilisation levels and expected new customer contracts, NEXTDC provides the
86	following guidance for FY24:
87	
88	 Total revenue in the range of A$400 million to A$415 million (unchanged)
89	
90	-  Net revenue in the range of A$296 million to A$304 million, up 6-9% on FY23 (unchanged)
91	
92	 Underlying EBITDA in the range of A$190 million to A$200 million (unchanged)
93	
94	 Capital expenditure in the range of A$850 million to A$900 million (unchanged)
95	
96	Commenting on the reaffirmed FY24 guidance, Mr Scroggie said:
97	
98	_“NEXTDC’s first half performance shows that the Company remains on track to achieve its key revenue and_
99	_underlying EBITDA targets. We expect to deliver another strong operating and financial performance in FY24 and_
100	_remain very well placed to support customer growth as well as take advantage of growth opportunities as they_
101	_arise.”_
102	
103	Authorised for release by the Board of NEXTDC Limited.
104	
105	**ENDS**
106	
107	For more information:
108	
109	**Simon Guzowski**
110	T: +61 2 8072 4943
111	[E: investorrelations@nextdc.com](mailto:investorrelations@nextdc.com)
112	
113	[NEXTDC Investor Centre: www.nextdc.com/our-company/investor-centre](http://www.nextdc.com/our-company/investor-centre)
114	
115	
116	-----
117	
118	### About NEXTDC
119	
120	NEXTDC is an ASX 100-listed technology company and Asia’s most innovative Data Centre-as-a-Service
121	provider. We are building the infrastructure platform for the digital economy, delivering the critical power,
122	security and connectivity for global cloud computing providers, enterprise and Government.
123	
124	NEXTDC is recognised globally for the design, construction and operation of Australia’s only network of
125	Uptime Institute certified Tier IV facilities, and one of only a few data centre operators in the Southern
126	Hemisphere to achieve Tier IV Gold certification for Operational Sustainability. NEXTDC has a strong focus
127	on sustainability and operational excellence through renewable energy sources and delivering world-class
128	operational efficiency. Our data centres have been engineered to deliver exceptional levels of efficiency and
129	the industry’s lowest Total Cost of Operation through NABERS 5-star energy efficiency.
130	
131	NEXTDC’s corporate operations have been certified carbon neutral under the Australian Government’s
132	_Climate Active Carbon Neutral Standard._
133	
134	Our Cloud Centre partner ecosystem is Australia’s most dynamic digital marketplace, comprising carriers,
135	cloud providers and IT service providers, enabling local and international customers to source and connect
136	with cloud platforms, service providers and vendors to build complex hybrid cloud networks and scale their
137	critical IT infrastructure services.
138	
139	NEXTDC is where the cloud lives®.
140	
141	[To learn more, visit www.nextdc.com](about:blank)
142	
143	
144	-----
145

2390	Management uses FFO as a supplemental performance measure because, in excluding real estate related depreciation and amortization and gains and
2391	losses from property dispositions and after adjustments for unconsolidated partnerships and joint ventures, it provides a performance measure that,
2392	when compared year over year, captures trends in occupancy rates, rental rates and operating costs. We also believe that, as a widely recognized
2393	measure of the performance of REITs, FFO will be used by investors as a basis to compare our operating performance with that of other REITs. However,
2394	because FFO excludes depreciation and amortization and captures neither the changes in the value of our data centers that result from use or market
2395	conditions, nor the level of capital expenditures and capitalized leasing commissions necessary to maintain the operating performance of our data
2396	centers, all of which have real economic effect and could materially impact our financial condition and results from operations, the utility of FFO as a
2397	measure of our performance is limited. Other REITs may not calculate FFO in accordance with the Nareit definition and, accordingly, our FFO may not be
2398	comparable to other REITs’ FFO. FFO should be considered only as a supplement to net income computed in accordance with GAAP as a measure of our
2399	performance.
2400	
2401	**Core Funds from Operations (Core FFO):**
2402	We present core funds from operations, or Core FFO, as a supplemental operating measure because, in excluding certain items that do not reflect core
2403	revenue or expense streams, it provides a performance measure that, when compared year over year, captures trends in our core business operating
2404	performance. We calculate Core FFO by adding to or subtracting from FFO (i) other non-core revenue adjustments, (ii) transaction and integration
2405	expenses, (iii) loss from early extinguishment of debt, (iv) gain on / issuance costs associated with redeemed preferred stock, (v) severance, equity
2406	acceleration and legal expenses, (vi) gain/loss on FX revaluation, and (vii) other non-core expense adjustments. Because certain of these adjustments
2407	have a real economic impact on our financial condition and results from operations, the utility of Core FFO as a measure of our performance is limited.
2408	Other REITs may calculate Core FFO differently than we do and accordingly, our Core FFO may not be comparable to other REITs’ Core FFO. Core FFO
2409	should be considered only as a supplement to net income computed in accordance with GAAP as a measure of our performance.
2410	
2411	**Adjusted Funds from Operations (AFFO):**
2412	We present adjusted funds from operations, or AFFO, as a supplemental operating measure because, when compared year over year, it assesses our
2413	ability to fund dividend and distribution requirements from our operating activities. We also believe that, as a widely recognized measure of the
2414	operations of REITs, AFFO will be used by investors as a basis to assess our ability to fund dividend payments in comparison to other REITs, including on
2415	a per share and unit basis. We calculate AFFO by adding to or subtracting from Core FFO (i) non-real estate depreciation, (ii) amortization of deferred
2416	financing costs, (iii) amortization of debt discount/premium, (iv) non-cash stock-based compensation expense, (v) straight-line rental revenue,
2417	(vi) straight-line rental expense, (vii) above- and below-market rent amortization, (viii) deferred tax expense / (benefit), (ix) leasing compensation and
2418	internal lease commissions, and (x) recurring capital expenditures. Other REITs may calculate AFFO differently than we do and, accordingly, our AFFO
2419	may not be comparable to other REITs’ AFFO. AFFO should be considered only as a supplement to net income computed in accordance with GAAP as a
2420	measure of our performance.
2421	
2422	**EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA:**
2423	We believe that earnings before interest, loss from early extinguishment of debt, income taxes, and depreciation and amortization, or EBITDA, and
2424	Adjusted EBITDA (as defined below), are useful supplemental performance measures because they allow investors to view our performance without the
2425	impact of non-cash depreciation and amortization or the cost of debt and, with respect to Adjusted EBITDA, (i) unconsolidated joint venture real estate
2426	related depreciation & amortization, (ii) unconsolidated joint venture interest expense and tax, (iii) severance, equity acceleration and legal expenses,
2427	(iv) transaction and integration expenses, (v) gain (loss) on sale / deconsolidation, (vi) provision for impairment, (vii) other non-core adjustments, net,
2428	(viii) non-controlling interests, (ix) preferred stock dividends, and (x) issuance costs associated with redeemed preferred stock. Adjusted EBITDA is
2429	EBITDA excluding (i) unconsolidated joint venture real estate related depreciation & amortization, (ii) unconsolidated joint venture interest expense and
2430	tax, (iii) severance, equity acceleration and legal expenses, (iv) transaction and integration expenses, (v) gain (loss) on sale / deconsolidation, (vi)
2431	provision for impairment, (vii) other non-core adjustments, net, (vii) non-controlling interests, (ix) preferred stock dividends, and (x) gain on / issuance
2432	costs associated with redeemed preferred stock. In addition, we believe EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA are frequently used by securities analysts,
2433	investors, and other interested parties in the evaluation of REITs. Because EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA are calculated before recurring cash charges
2434	including interest expense and income taxes, exclude capitalized costs, such as leasing commissions, and are not adjusted for capital expenditures or
2435	other recurring cash requirements of our business, their utility as a measure of our performance is limited. Other REITs may calculate EBITDA and
2436	Adjusted EBITDA differently than we do and, accordingly, our EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA may not be comparable to other REITs’ EBITDA and Adjusted
2437	EBITDA. Accordingly, EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA should be considered only as supplements to net income computed in accordance with GAAP as a
2438	measure of our financial performance.
2439
